title: Wait-for / detect higher-RAM Pod hosts for LTX (same-price RAM configs vary by availability; LTX warm perf needs ~90GB)
description:
PROBLEM (found 2026-06-30 during LTX GGUF Pod testing): LTX-2.3 warm-gen perf is RAM-GATED, but the picker can't help the user land a high-RAM host. The picker shows ONE RAM number per GPU, from `lowestPrice.minMemory` (routes/runpodRemote.js gpuTypes() ~line 116-137). The picker reads `lowestPrice(input:{gpuCount,dataCenterId,secureCloud}).minMemory`.

USER OBSERVATION (the accurate model, refined across the session): in ONE data center (EU-RO-1) the user has connected to RTX 5090 hosts with BOTH 60GB and ~90GB RAM, several times each. So RunPod genuinely has MULTIPLE RAM configs for the same GPU in the same DC. CRITICAL refinement (user): RunPod's price is FIXED PER CARD — the RTX 5090 is the same $/hr regardless of RAM config OR data center. So price does NOT vary by RAM, which means `lowestPrice` is NOT hiding a pricier high-RAM host behind a cheaper one (they're price-tied). The most likely behavior: with prices tied, `lowestPrice.minMemory` reflects whatever host config is AVAILABLE at query time — so it reads 60GB when only 60GB hosts are free and 90GB when a 90GB host is free. The number is a LIVE availability signal, not a fixed floor. (Earlier framings 'our UI hides a tier', 'different DC', 'fixed floor', and 'pricier host hidden' were all PARTIAL/WRONG — the truth: multiple same-price RAM configs exist; the API surfaces whichever is currently available.)

WHY IT MATTERS: working set = ~14.5GB fp8 Gemma encoder + ~15GB Q8 GGUF transformer + VAEs + ComfyUI/torch/OS overhead ≈ 40-45GB. Both Gemma (fp8) and the transformer (Q8) are already at their QUALITY FLOOR (fp4 Gemma 'hurts', fp8 transformer rejected for eyes/teeth — see docs/builder/research/model-set.md + quant-and-coldstart-investigation.md), so the working set can't shrink. On a ~60GB host the RAM cache thrashes → weights re-fault from disk every gen → warm gens balloon to ~2:14+ (vs ~22-25s on a ~90GB host). ~90GB is the effective floor for good LTX warm perf.

RESEARCH SPIKE FIRST (since price is flat, RAM is purely an availability signal — confirm how the API surfaces it): (1) Does `lowestPrice.minMemory` for the 5090 in EU-RO-1 actually CHANGE over time (read 60 sometimes, 90 sometimes) as hosts free up? Poll it to confirm it's a live availability value, not a constant. (2) Is there an offerings/availability query that lists ALL currently-available RAM configs at once (so we could show '60GB or 90GB available now')? (3) Does the deploy/create call let you request a minimum system RAM (so RunPod only assigns a ≥90GB host)? If (1) yes, lever B works by polling; if (3) yes, B is deterministic instead of poll-and-hope.

LEVERS (by feasibility):
- A. POST-CONNECT DETECT + WARN (always works, no API dependency): read the connected Pod's actual total RAM (wrapper /wrapper/stats — confirm it reports total system RAM, add if missing), and when under a configurable LTX floor (~90GB) show a soft warning ('this Pod has XGB RAM; LTX warm gens may be slow — reconnect to roll for a higher-RAM host'). Cheapest, ship first.
- B. WAIT-FOR-HIGH-RAM (the real prize — feasible because price is flat, so no cost downside to waiting for the bigger host): reuse the EXISTING 'Auto-retry connection (wait for out-of-stock GPU)' pattern in Settings — add a 'require ≥N GB RAM' option that polls `lowestPrice.minMemory` (or the offerings query) and holds-and-retries connect until a ≥90GB 5090 is available, then deploys. Same $/hr either way, so the only cost is wait time. Reuses proven retry plumbing.
- C. PICKER HINT: show 'recommended ≥90GB RAM for LTX' next to the RAM number; if the API exposes currently-available tiers, list them.

FILES: routes/runpodRemote.js gpuTypes() (lowestPrice query ~line 116-137) + the create/availability queries; the wrapper stats path (mpi-ci/cubric-vision-pod/wrapper.py /wrapper/stats — total system RAM?); routes/remoteModels.js / comfyController (read the stat); the Settings remote-engine UI (RAM display, the auto-retry toggle area) + status bar.

BRANCH: RunPod. DEFERRED — logged at user request during the LTX showcase session; do NOT address inline. Spike the API (offerings/minMemory constraint) before scoping B/C; A is shippable regardless.
